title: Question: inline all sub-functions within _eval greatly improve the performance with GCC 4.8.1
body:
---


Author Name: **Jie Xu** (@jiexu)
Original Redmine Message: 1525 from https://www.veripool.org



---

when trying to improve the performance of verilator model, I did some callgrind analysis on one test model.

```
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
            Ir
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
1,083,335,590  PROGRAM TOTALS

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
          Ir  file:function
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
202,937,588  generated/debug/Vsim_top.cpp:Vsim_top::_eval(Vsim_top__Syms*)
 76,848,704  generated/debug/Vsim_top.cpp:Vsim_top::_combo__TOP__330(Vsim_top__Syms*) 
 72,403,472  generated/debug/Vsim_top.cpp:Vsim_top::_change_request(Vsim_top__Syms*) 
 55,248,836  generated/debug/Vsim_top.cpp:Vsim_top::_combo__TOP__252(Vsim_top__Syms*) 
 50,653,271  generated/debug/Vsim_top.cpp:Vsim_top::_combo__TOP__361(Vsim_top__Syms*) 
 41,502,198  generated/debug/Vsim_top.cpp:Vsim_top::_combo__TOP__296(Vsim_top__Syms*) 
 36,028,065  generated/debug/Vsim_top.cpp:Vsim_top::_combo__TOP__363(Vsim_top__Syms*) 
 34,826,300  generated/debug/Vsim_top.cpp:Vsim_top::_combo__TOP__278(Vsim_top__Syms*) 
 24,283,790  generated/debug/Vsim_top.cpp:Vsim_top::_combo__TOP__332(Vsim_top__Syms*) 
 19,566,250  generated/debug/Vsim_top.cpp:Vsim_top::_combo__TOP__381(Vsim_top__Syms*) 
 19,304,864  generated/debug/Vsim_top.cpp:Vsim_top::_combo__TOP__352(Vsim_top__Syms*) 

```
Surprisingly the @_eval@ function itself actually takes a lot of CPU cycles. Then I tried to make all the functions which are called by @_eval()@ as inline functions. The performance of the test model boosted about 17% while the executable sizes were almost the same.

Based on this, I think we probably should have an option to make all the @_combo, _sequent, _multiclk@ function as inline as default. I think this is safe for all C++ compilers since those functions are called by only one place. Inlining those functions won't increase the executable size but will make functions like @_eval()@ much less costly.
